snippet.title: Episode 5 | Den største testen
snippet.description:
Deltakerne får møte konsernsjefen i Equinor, Anders Opedal – og stille ham de vanskelige spørsmålene: Er det forsvarlig å fortsette med olje og gass? Hvorfor går ikke omstillingen raskere? Og tror Opedal selv at vi når klimamålene?

00:00 Intro
00:13 Vignett
00:46 Deltakernes erfaringer fra Den Største Testen 
01:52 Konsernsjef i Equinor Anders Opedal 
02:40 Forventninger til møtet 
02:57 Møtet med Opedal 
03:45 Opedal: Balansert overgang fra olje og gass  
08:37 Opedal: Investeringer i fornybar 
09:48 Opedal: Equinors ambisjon om netto null i 2050  
11:11 Opedal: hvorfor det grønne skiftet tar tid  
16:39 Deltakerne om det viktigste de har lært og hva de selv kan gjøre 
17:52 Opedal: 1,5 gradersmålet 
18:53 Deltakernes erfaringer fra Den Største Testen

snippet.title: Den største testen
snippet.description: Møt fire unge som skal få utforske og utfordre Equinors omstillingsplan. På reisen skal de få lære om ulike energiløsninger, sparre med fageksperter og til slutt møte sjefen sjøl, Anders Opedal. Er planen god nok? Vil verden, Norge og Equinor være godt nok rustet til å bestå “Den største testen”?

snippet.title: Energy Transition and Security: Meeting Demand in a Volatile World
snippet.description:
Energy Transition and Security: Meeting Demand in a Volatile World

A global energy crisis caused by fuel shortages led to blackouts, energy shortages, and rising electricity costs across Europe and Asia this Winter. Politicians are calling for a transition to green energy alternatives and pledging to reduce dependence on oil and gas, but many countries lack the infrastructure to harness renewable energy for mass consumption in the near future. What role can producer nations play in ensuring the world has adequate and affordable supplies of fuel while countries work towards building the infrastructure needed for a transition to more renewable sources of energy? How can suppliers work together to regulate prices and manage supply, while protecting their interests in a world increasingly committed to green energy? 

H.E. Saad Sherida Al-Kaabi, Minister of State for Energy Affairs; President and CEO of QatarEnergy
Patrick Pouyanné, Chairman And CEO, TotalEnergies
Anders Opedal, President and CEO, Equinor
Hadley Gamble Reporter, CNBC (moderator)

localized.title: Energy Transition and Security: Meeting Demand in a Volatile World
localized.description:
Energy Transition and Security: Meeting Demand in a Volatile World

A global energy crisis caused by fuel shortages led to blackouts, energy shortages, and rising electricity costs across Europe and Asia this Winter. Politicians are calling for a transition to green energy alternatives and pledging to reduce dependence on oil and gas, but many countries lack the infrastructure to harness renewable energy for mass consumption in the near future. What role can producer nations play in ensuring the world has adequate and affordable supplies of fuel while countries work towards building the infrastructure needed for a transition to more renewable sources of energy? How can suppliers work together to regulate prices and manage supply, while protecting their interests in a world increasingly committed to green energy? 

H.E. Saad Sherida Al-Kaabi, Minister of State for Energy Affairs; President and CEO of QatarEnergy
Patrick Pouyanné, Chairman And CEO, TotalEnergies
Anders Opedal, President and CEO, Equinor
Hadley Gamble Reporter, CNBC (moderator)

snippet.title: Episode 4 | Hva er egentlig greia med lavkarbonløsninger?
snippet.description:
Samfunnet skal elektrifiseres, men i noen industrier er ikke dette mulig. Hvordan skal vi da klare å nå klimamålene? Hvilke løsninger må på plass, og hvordan ligger vi an?

snippet.title: BP makes profits of £7 billion as energy bills soar | 5 News
snippet.description:
► Have you subscribed to 5 News?: http://bit.ly/5NewsSub 

► BP have made record profits of £7 billion in the past three months. The profits mean they will be paying an extra £695 million in windfall taxes this year but there are calls for it to be much more. 

With the cost-of-living crisis and the rising cost of fuel, the record profits have left a sour taste in some people's mouths who are struggling to make ends meet. 
 
► Follow 5 News on Twitter to keep up to date with the latest news - https://twitter.com/5_News

Message us on Facebook to share your opinion - https://www.facebook.com/c5News
